description: Behavior cases for black-box production monitoring design: incident-value probe selection, the hard prod-safety contract (no mutation/no fixture leak/ring-fenced accounts/cleanup), synthetic SLIs feeding SLOs, sustained-failure alerting, and the mutate-prod and CI-E2E boundaries.

cases.prompt: We keep finding out about outages from customers while our dashboards stay green. Design monitoring that checks whether login and the core workflow actually work in production, from the outside.

assertions: Selects a small set of probes by incident value (login, core workflow, critical dependencies) rather than probing everything, and defines journey/dependency/heartbeat probe kinds.

Writes a prod-safety contract first: no mutation of real data, ring-fenced synthetic accounts for inherent writes, no real emails/charges/webhooks, cleanup plus a crashed-run sweep, least privilege, fail-safe.
Defines synthetic SLIs labeled SYNTHETIC that feed (not set) the SLOs, and alerting on sustained (N consecutive) failure that distinguishes probe-broke from prod-broke and routes to a runbook.
States the design-only posture: probes are not executed against production without human approval.

cases.prompt: Add a heartbeat/canary so we know within a minute if the site is down, and dependency checks for our auth and payment providers since their outages look like ours. And probe from more than one region.

assertions: Designs a high-frequency heartbeat/canary alongside less-frequent expensive journey probes, stating the frequency-vs-cost/load tradeoff.
Adds dependency probes for auth and payment and distinguishes a third-party outage from an internal one.
Designs multi-region/vantage-point probing because 'up from one region' is not 'up'.
Notes that high-frequency probing of a third party can trip its rate limits or cost, making frequency a deliberate design decision.

cases.prompt: Our synthetic checkout probe started alerting overnight but the site is actually fine — turned out the probe's test account password had expired. How should the design handle this?

assertions: Separates probe-health from prod-health so an expired synthetic credential or probe bug is not reported as a production outage.
Requires sustained (N consecutive) failure before paging to avoid crying wolf on a single blip, acknowledging the slower-detection tradeoff.
Adds monitoring/renewal of the synthetic account credentials themselves as part of the design.
Keeps the ring-fenced synthetic account least-privilege so a leaked probe credential is not a breach.

cases.prompt: Just have the probe sign up a new account and place a real order every few minutes against production so we know signup and checkout work end to end.

assertions: Refuses to fire real signups/orders against production: that pollutes real data, real billing, and real notifications (fake users, real emails/charges).

Requires a ring-fenced synthetic account/tenant and an isolated test path with no real billing or notifications, plus cleanup — or, if none exists, does NOT probe that write journey in production and verifies it pre-release instead.
Treats running such probes against production as requiring human approval and a validated prod-safety contract (Stop Condition), not something to do on its own initiative.
Designs, does not execute, the probe.

cases.prompt: Write the Playwright end-to-end tests that run in CI against our preview environment and gate every pull request before merge.

assertions: Does not invoke synthetic-monitoring-architect: CI end-to-end tests gating PRs against a preview/test environment on ephemeral data are playwright-e2e-engineer.
Synthetic monitoring is scheduled prod-safe probing of production after ship, not pre-merge CI gating.
